# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Publish Release Action  # Name of your workflow (you should change this name)  
on:    # List of actions that will be used in our job    
  release:             # What event triggers the GitHub Actions flow     
tags:                 # Array for triggering events by tags       
 - 'v*'               # Tags which would make it run your workflow      
secretmanager.actions@latest    # Using latest version of secret manager actions         
jobs:     # List of jobs that will be used in our flow     
  build_and_publish_:   # Job name (same as above)          
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest        # Which runner to use      
    steps:         # Array for the commands           
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2          # Step that checks out your repository allowing you access its files    
      - id: setup_node                    # ID of step which is used by our next setps   (can be anything) 
        with:                           # Variables to pass into the steps   
            node-version @latest       # Node version, defaulting it latest LTS if not specified in job        
      - name: Cache NPM packages           # Name of step             
        uses: actions/cache@v2          # Action for caching npm dependencies   (we'll use this to speed up our next steps) 
        with:                 .            # Path on your runner where you want GitHub Actions cache directories stored    
         key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}       # Key for caching folder to be used as a directory name   
      - npm ci                        # Run the command below (npm install in your case)   if there's any changes it will update    
        with:                      .          # Path where package lock file is situated  and you want GitHub Action cache directories stored.      
      - run: |                       # Command to be executed, can also include steps    for multiple commands at once (comma separated values)   using "|" symbol          `npm ci; npm publish --access public'         }, { with : . }     , 'run:' ${{ runner.os }} '-> master - Action: Publish Release', if not tag => exit 1, tags => $tags
